summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a May ’77 show with a smoking Sugaree, Jack‑A‑Roe and Bertha, plus a standout second set.
review: The recording is an exceptionally clean soundboard mix that lets the hall ambience shine while keeping every instrument in focus. Musically the band is tight and energetic, delivering highlight performances of Sugaree, Jack‑A‑Roe, High Time and a fiery second‑set Bertha; even the less‑favored Sunrise sits comfortably in an otherwise flawless set. Reviewers repeatedly praise Jerry’s soaring leads, the balanced interplay with Bob and Phil, and the overall excitement of this late‑May 1977 outing.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews uniformly rate 4–5 stars, praising both musical performance and the pristine soundboard mix; only minor criticism of a single song (Sunrise) appears.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with excellent hall ambience; reviewers note the recording is “remarkable”, “exceptional mixdown”, and “as good as it gets”. No complaints of drop‑outs or muffiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A slightly unconventional set list for May ’77, with a solid first half and an explosively hot second set; the band stays tight throughout despite a late audience arrival.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his scorching leads on Sugaree, Bertha and Jack‑A‑Roe; described as “orgasmic” and “going for it”.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer suggested Phil could be a touch louder in the mix but otherwise noted he’s solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted as being very audible and on‑point; reviewers love hearing the Bob‑Jerry‑Phil interplay.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards are mentioned as sitting well in the mix (especially on Sugaree) and adding depth.
